title: GrowGeneration Corp Q3 2021 Earnings Call Transcript
text: GrowGeneration delivered strong Q3 2021 results with revenue of $116 million, representing a 111% year-over-year increase. The company achieved same-store sales growth of 15.7% and gross profit margins expanded 290 basis points to 29.4%. Net income reached $4 million ($0.07 per share), while adjusted EBITDA grew 63% to $10.8 million. The company ended the quarter with $93 million in cash and marketable securities. Despite the strong quarter, management acknowledged significant headwinds. CEO Darren Lampert noted that California, Colorado, and Oregon are experiencing cannabis oversupply, creating downward pressure on the cost per pound of flower. This prompted the company to adjust full-year guidance to $435-440 million in revenue and $41-43 million in adjusted EBITDA. Management outlined five key growth drivers for 2022: greenfield expansion into 15-20 new locations, primarily in emerging East Coast and Midwest markets; implementation of new enterprise resource planning systems using Oracle and Manhattan Associates; establishment of regional distribution center networks; expansion of private label brands from 9% to 25% of sales by 2023; and integration of three e-commerce platforms into a unified system. The company's proprietary brands Power Si and Char Coir are gaining traction, with Power Si now available in over 500 independent hydroponic locations across the U.S. and internationally.
